reasoning: The Manifold market's historical probability on Feb 20, 2025, was 21%. The resolution criteria specify the Bureau of Labor Statistics, which correlates with the CPI (Consumer Price Index). January 2025 CPI data (released Feb 12) was 3.0% YoY, which is well above the 2.5% threshold. While the January PCE data (released Feb 28) cooled slightly to a 2.5% annual rate, CPI inherently runs higher than PCE. Furthermore, expectations over the next year (e.g., Fannie Mae and others) project CPI remaining around 2.8% to 2.9% in Q4 2025 under current policy paths. Current trends and widely anticipated inflationary policies (like tariffs) under Trump make it unlikely CPI drops below 2.5% by the end of 2025. Hence, my probability remains closely aligned with the recent market estimate at 20%.
